channel.title: Simon Lightwood MP
channel.description: Labour and Co-operative MP for Wakefield and Rothwell.

item.title: 50 new buses for Wakefield
item.description: Great to join Arriva Yorkshire in Wakefield today as they unveiled the first of their brand‑new, Yorkshire‑built buses -part of a £340m investment to modernise their UK fleet, with £15m of that spent right here at home Wakefield is the first to benefit, with 50 new lower‑emission buses already entering service. It’s a real boost for passengers and a sign of the progress being made to improve public transport locally. There’s still more to do before services are where our communities rightly...
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Serenity Beauty & Skin Therapies (February 2026)
item.description: A huge well done to Serenity Beauty & Skin Therapies on Leeds Road, my Small Business of the Month winner for February! Nominations spoke about how every visit feels special, and how consistently high the standard of service is for everyone who walks through the door. Huge congratulations to Abbie. From Saturday girl to business owner, Abbie’s doing a great job
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Should we ban social media for under-16's?
item.description: I’m really keen to hear your views on an issue many parents and young people have raised with me: how we can help young people build a healthier, safer relationship with phones and social media. The Government has launched a national conversation on children’s online safety, recognising both the huge benefits of technology and the growing concerns about its impact on young people’s wellbeing.
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Happy birthday Harry!
item.description: Wakefield WW2 Veteran Celebrates 100th Birthday with Visit from Local MP and Messages from the King and Prime Minister A much‑loved Wakefield resident and Second World War veteran has celebrated his 100th birthday with a series of heartfelt tributes recognising his remarkable life and service. Harry Birdsall, who served in the Royal Army Service Corps and helped evacuate prisoners from the Bergen‑Belsen concentration camp in 1945, marked his milestone birthday surrounded by family, friends,...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Bradley's Cafe (January 2026)
item.description: My January Small Business of the Month is Bradley's Café on Wood St! Entries from customers said going to Bradley's is like 'popping into a friend's house for lunch' and 'they really take the time to remember and get to know ever customer'. Elisa and the team do a marvellous job - what an asset to Wakefield. Pop in for a cuppa or a sarnie when you're next in the area
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: It’s time for a national conversation about social media and our children
item.description: I’m a dad, and like any parent, I spend a lot of time worrying about my kids. However, in the digital age, with more services moving online and smartphones, tablets and computers part of our daily lives, these worries now include making sure they are safe online. Many of you have contacted me about the effects that the constant connectivity, online content and social media use can have on children's wellbeing, concentration, sleep and relationships. Our children should be able to spend more...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Nel's Natural Raw Food (December 2025)
item.description: The entries described their brilliant customer service and a real passion for keeping the furriest members of our families happy and healthy. Nell’s is a fantastic example of a local business going the extra mile - from expert advice to high‑quality natural products, they’re helping pet owners across our community give their animals the very best. It’s always a pleasure to shine a spotlight on small businesses like this, who work incredibly hard and play such an important role in our local...
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Reflecting on 2025
item.description: As we begin the new year, I want to take a moment to reflect on all that has been achieved in 2025, the first full calendar year of this Labour Government, and to look ahead to what we can accomplish together in 2026. The General Election may feel like only yesterday, but already this Government has delivered transformative change for communities here in Wakefield and Rothwell. Over the past twelve months, we have passed crucial pieces of legislation that will make a real difference to...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Wakefield city centre needs a Post Office.
item.description: The Post Office branch at Trinity Walk is set to close at the end of February, following a decision by TG Jones, the business where the branch is currently located. ​ I am working closely with Wakefield Council, the Post Office, Wakefield BID, and other local organisations to fight for a continued Post Office presence in the city centre - where it belongs. I am also extremely concerned about the impact this decision will have on branch staff, especially so close to Christmas, their busiest...
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Ross's Barber Shop (November 2025)
item.description: November's winner is Ross's Barber Shop on Woodland Crescent in Rothwell. Jodie is carrying on the proud legacy of her grandad, Ross. She's now been running the place for nearly 15 years. From championing other local businesses to cutting hair for the homeless, Jodie goes above and beyond for her customers and community.
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: A Budget that delivers for Wakefield and Rothwell
item.description: Last week, the Chancellor delivered her Budget, which carried a strong message: Britain’s future will be built on fairness, not austerity. Gone are the days of cuts that hollowed out our communities or promises that couldn’t be paid for. Instead, this was a plan rooted in renewal, investing in people, in families and in the places we call home. But what does that really mean for us here in Wakefield and Rothwell? Let’s look beyond the headlines and into the lives behind the numbers. For too...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Small business of the month
item.description: Small businesses in Wakefield and Rothwell are the heartbeat of our local economy. They bring innovation, character, and opportunity to our high streets and communities. Every month, I’ll be celebrating one local business as Small Business of the Month - shining a spotlight on their resilience, creativity, and the vital role they play in driving growth and supporting local jobs. Their success is our success, and they deserve to be recognised and championed. Do you know a brilliant small...
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Honouring our veterans
item.description: Remembrance Sunday and Armistice Day are opportunities for us to remember the service and sacrifice of all those who fought to defend our freedoms and protect our way of life. And although they happen every year, these important dates never lose their importance and significance to our history and our national story. From both World Wars to Kosovo and Afghanistan, our veterans have stood up for our country with courage and sacrifice. Their service has kept us safe and we must never forget...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: A Britain Built for all
item.description: For many people, party conferences can feel a world away from everyday life. Four days of speeches, resolutions, and political jargon -...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Love Lupset
item.description: After years of being left out, Lupset is finally getting the support it needs - thanks to our Labour government. ​ I’ve worked hard to get £20 million for Lupset. This money will be given over 10 years, which means £2 million every year to make our area better. Read more
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Recognition of the State of Palestine
item.description: The State of Palestine On 21 September 2025, the UK Government made a historic decision: to formally recognise the State of Palestine....
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Next Stop: Better Buses
item.description: There’s no sugar-coating it - bus services across Wakefield and Rothwell haven’t been up to scratch for a long time. I hear about it...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Turning the tide on polluters
item.description: After 14 years of Conservative failure on sewage, Labour is cleaning up the mess and reforming our water system to work better for...
item.dc:creator: Simon Lightwood

item.title: Our NHS is broken - but not beaten
item.description: Every single week, one issue comes up more than any other—the state of our NHS. And for me, this isn’t just political, it’s personal....
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8

item.title: Better Buses Bill
item.description: Over the past month, I’ve had the privilege of leading the Government’s Bus Services Bill through its Committee Stage in Parliament. This...
item.dc:creator: elliecjames8
